score_injection_action: Rank=Disgraced with close_fail_qa_pass(-12) in recent events confirms prior over-permissive BA-validation passes; this run applies aggressive scrutiny to all 5 dimensions with particular attention to scope completeness and AC literal text vs execution drift — no rubber-stamping.

qa.overall_assessment: BA spec has 4 concrete defects: (1) AC2 incorrectly assigns BA-QA fail gates to agents/ba.md (they belong in agents/qa.md only, per spec Section 5); (2) AC5 is missing the third required registration location — prompt-workflow.py agent_types list — that BA's own Technical Hints establish as mandatory via the test-writer precedent; (3) AC10 self-contradicts by saying 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' while simultaneously listing .env/credentials/keys/logs which are absent from that reference; (4) commands/dev-overnight.md is absent from all affected-files lists despite the spec declaring '/dev-overnight: enabled'. These are blocking defects that will cause either incorrect implementation or unverifiable acceptance criteria.

objections.title: AC2 incorrectly assigns BA-QA fail gates to agents/ba.md
objections.description: AC2's THEN clause states: 'agents/ba.md contains the 6-step Reference Resolution procedure AND the list of implicit reference trigger words AND the three new BA-QA fail gates'. The three new BA-QA fail gates (candidate_anchors ignored, missing Reference Resolution, counter-evidence absent) are QA-mode validation rules, not BA procedure rules. Spec Section 5 places them under 'BA-QA validation gains new fail gates' — meaning they are criteria that QA applies when validating BA output. AC3 correctly assigns them to agents/qa.md. AC2 adding them to ba.md creates a double-assignment: dev will place fail-gate text in ba.md (satisfying AC2) AND in qa.md (satisfying AC3), but the spec only requires them in qa.md. Worse, if dev puts them only in ba.md to satisfy AC2, AC3 will fail because ba.md is not the right file. The AC2 literal text is wrong and will produce either duplicate placement or implementation confusion.
objections.evidence: Spec Section 5 lines 92-95: 'BA-QA validation gains new fail gates: BA ignores structural_context.candidate_anchors → FAIL; BA has no Reference Resolution when implicit references detected → FAIL; BA only proves its initial interpretation without listing counter-evidence → FAIL'. These are validation rules for QA, not procedural rules for BA. They parallel agents/qa.md's existing BA-Validation Mode section. AC3 already correctly places them in qa.md.
objections.required_fix: Remove 'AND the three new BA-QA fail gates' from AC2's THEN clause. AC2 should only require the 6-step Reference Resolution procedure and implicit reference trigger words in agents/ba.md. The fail gates belong exclusively in AC3 (agents/qa.md).

objections.title: AC5 missing third registration location: prompt-workflow.py agent_types list
objections.description: AC5 verifies 'graphify' in CP_AGENTS (pretool-cp-checkin.py) and ALLOWED_AGENTS (spec-check.py) but omits the third required location established by BA's own Technical Hints. The Technical Hints state verbatim: 'Existing pattern for agent registration (both sets at once): test-writer was added to CP_AGENTS (hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py:72) and ALLOWED_AGENTS (scripts/spec-check.py:59) and agent_types list (hooks/prompt-workflow.py:598) together'. Confirmed: hooks/prompt-workflow.py:598 contains agent_types = [..., 'test-writer', ...] which creates the per-session dev-registry sentinel file (agent.json) that every subagent reads as its FIRST ACTION. Without 'graphify' in agent_types, the graphify sentinel is never created, the FIRST ACTION Read in the graphify subagent fails with file-not-found, and pretool-cp-checkin.py cannot map the subagent UUID to its agent_type. This is a runtime failure, not a cosmetic gap. BA identified the precedent but failed to include it in AC5.
objections.evidence: BA Technical Hints section: 'test-writer was added to CP_AGENTS (hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py:72) and ALLOWED_AGENTS (scripts/spec-check.py:59) and agent_types list (hooks/prompt-workflow.py:598) together'. Verified: hooks/prompt-workflow.py line 598 contains agent_types list; 'test-writer' is present; 'graphify' is absent. R5 requirements decomposition only references pretool-cp-checkin.py and spec-check.py.
objections.required_fix: Add a third THEN clause to AC5: 'graphify' appears in agent_types list in hooks/prompt-workflow.py (line 598 region). This ensures the dev-registry sentinel file for graphify is created on every /dev invocation, enabling the FIRST ACTION registration protocol.

objections.title: AC10 self-contradicts: 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' is incompatible with 'covering .env, credentials, keys, logs'
objections.description: AC10 states: 'graphify_lib.py defines EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS tuple including .env, credentials, keys, logs, .git, venv patterns (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35)'. The actual EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS at blast-radius-tool.py:35 contains: '/venv/', '/worktrees/', '/.archive/', '/plugins/', '/.git/', '/__pycache__/', '/node_modules/'. It does NOT contain .env, credentials, keys, or logs. If dev 'mirrors' the reference pattern, the grep check for '.env' and 'credentials' in AC10 will fail. If dev adds those items to satisfy the literal list, the implementation diverges from the stated reference. The spec Section 5 Risk item 7 says 'Sensitive data in graph cache — exclude .env, credentials, keys, logs' which is the actual security requirement. The AC10 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' reference is misleading because the reference does not contain the security-sensitive patterns. Dev will be confused about which patterns to include.
objections.evidence: Verified blast-radius-tool.py lines 35-42: EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS = ('/venv/', '/worktrees/', '/.archive/', '/plugins/', '/.git/', '/__pycache__/', '/node_modules/'). No .env, credentials, keys, or logs. AC10's assertion that these items come from 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' is factually wrong.
objections.required_fix: Rewrite AC10 to separate the two requirements: (a) graphify_lib.py defines EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS covering security-sensitive files: .env, credentials, keys, logs, .git, venv (per spec Section 5 Risk item 7 and arch-8); (b) the LOCK_EX pattern mirrors spec-check.py:171 (for AC7, not AC10). Remove the 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' attribution from AC10 — the reference pattern is for file-scope filtering (build artifacts), not sensitive-data exclusion.

objections.title: commands/dev-overnight.md absent from all affected-files lists despite spec declaring '/dev-overnight: enabled'
objections.description: Spec Section 5 Command Coverage states: '/dev-overnight: enabled, shares global update'. commands/dev-overnight.md exists (confirmed) and has its own independent orchestration step sequence (Steps 1-21) that does NOT inherit from commands/dev.md. The spec saying '/dev-overnight: enabled' requires either (a) commands/dev-overnight.md receives graphify insertion points in its Steps 8/12 (parallel BA/Dev phases), or (b) the spec's 'shares global update' means dev-overnight only benefits from graphify_lib.py infrastructure without explicit orchestrator-level insertion. BA did not investigate which interpretation is correct and made no determination. commands/dev-overnight.md is absent from PR2 affected files list and the context JSON files_to_modify list. If interpretation (a) is correct, this is a 33-file gap (the spec says 32 but overnight.md would be the 33rd). If interpretation (b) is correct, cp-07 ('Verify /dev-overnight enabled') has no concrete AC to satisfy.
objections.evidence: Spec Section 5: '/dev-overnight: enabled, shares global update'. Confirmed: commands/dev-overnight.md exists with its own Steps 1-21 workflow (grep confirmed). BA context JSON files_to_modify: ['settings.json', 'scripts/todo/dev.py', 'scripts/todo/dev-command.py', 'commands/dev.md', 'commands/dev-command.md', 'commands/redev.md', 'agents/ba.md', 'agents/qa.md', 'agents/dev.md'] — no commands/dev-overnight.md. BA ticket PR2 affected files: same list, no overnight.md.
objections.required_fix: BA must investigate and document: does '/dev-overnight: enabled, shares global update' require explicit insertion into commands/dev-overnight.md's Step 8 (BA dispatch) and Step 12 (Dev dispatch)? If yes, add commands/dev-overnight.md to the PR2 affected-files list and create a corresponding AC. If no (overnight inherits through agents/ modifications only), document this explicitly in the Technical Hints and add a test to AC5/cp-07 verifying the 'shared' mechanism.

objections.title: cp-07 command coverage checkpoint has no corresponding AC in the BA ticket
objections.description: The cp-state for this spec (cp-state-qa.json) has checkpoint cp-07: 'Verify command coverage: /dev and /dev-overnight and /redev enabled; /clean disabled; /pull triggers update'. The BA ticket has 11 ACs (AC1-AC11) but none of them verify the command coverage table. The spec Section 5 has an explicit Command Coverage section (/dev, /dev-overnight, /redev, /refactor, /clean, /pull) that constitutes a user-need clause. BA translated most spec requirements into ACs but omitted command coverage verification. This means cp-07 will have nothing to check against, and no acceptance criterion will enforce that all 5 commands (plus /pull) are correctly configured.
objections.evidence: cp-state-qa.json checkpoint list: cp-07 action='Verify command coverage: /dev and /dev-overnight and /redev enabled; /clean disabled; /pull triggers update'. BA ticket ACs AC1-AC11: none verify command coverage. Spec Section 5 Command Coverage section is explicit: 5 commands with specific behavior requirements.
objections.required_fix: Add AC12: Command Coverage — GIVEN all PR2 and PR3 files applied; WHEN reading commands/dev.md, commands/dev-command.md, commands/redev.md, commands/pull.md and settings.json; THEN (a) commands/dev.md has graphify enabled (Step 1.5 + Step 7.5), (b) commands/redev.md inherits /dev graphify behavior, (c) commands/pull.md has post-pull graphify-maintain.py update trigger, (d) no graphify insertion in /clean.

evidence_quality.notes: BA's factual claims are well-evidenced. Baseline grep results (zero graphify hits), CP_AGENTS verification (lines 60-77), ALLOWED_AGENTS verification (lines 45-60), fcntl.LOCK_EX at spec-check.py:171, EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS at blast-radius-tool.py:35, agent_types at prompt-workflow.py:598 — all confirmed accurate by direct file reads.

scope_alignment.notes: AC2 incorrectly assigns BA-QA fail gates to agents/ba.md (OBJ-1). No universal-scope-claim narrowing violations found for the Must Have requirements.

investigation_completeness.notes: AC5 missing the prompt-workflow.py agent_types third location (OBJ-2). cp-07 has no corresponding AC (OBJ-5). dev-overnight.md ambiguity uninvestigated (OBJ-4).

affected_file_accuracy.notes: commands/dev-overnight.md confirmed to exist (ls verified) but absent from all affected-files lists despite spec declaring it enabled (OBJ-4). All other files in the affected list exist and contain the structures BA claims.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: AC10 literal text says 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' but that reference does not contain .env/credentials/keys/logs patterns. Dev following the reference would fail AC10's own literal check (OBJ-3).

root_cause_verification.rationale: BA correctly identifies absence of graphify infrastructure as the root cause. All baseline verifications are accurate.

all_findings.issue: AC2 assigns BA-QA fail gates to agents/ba.md — they belong only in agents/qa.md (AC3). Will cause incorrect implementation or untestable state.
all_findings.recommendation: Remove 'AND the three new BA-QA fail gates' from AC2 THEN clause. Keep fail gates in AC3 only.

all_findings.issue: AC5 missing third registration site: hooks/prompt-workflow.py agent_types list. Without it, graphify subagent FIRST ACTION Read will fail at runtime.
all_findings.recommendation: Add 'graphify' in agent_types list (hooks/prompt-workflow.py:598) as a third THEN clause in AC5.

all_findings.issue: AC10 says 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' but that reference lacks .env/credentials/keys/logs. Dev cannot satisfy both the reference and the literal pattern list simultaneously.
all_findings.recommendation: Separate AC10 into security-sensitive exclusion (spec risk 7 + arch-8) vs build-artifact exclusion (blast-radius pattern). Remove the false 'mirroring' attribution.

all_findings.issue: commands/dev-overnight.md absent from all affected-files lists. Spec says /dev-overnight enabled but no concrete AC or file modification plan exists for it.
all_findings.recommendation: Investigate and document whether dev-overnight needs explicit orchestrator-level graphify insertion. Add affected-file entry or explicit rationale for exclusion.

all_findings.issue: No AC covers the spec's Command Coverage table (/dev, /dev-overnight, /redev, /clean, /pull). cp-07 checkpoint has nothing to verify against.
all_findings.recommendation: Add AC12 for command coverage verification.

failures.issue: Scope misassignment — BA-QA fail gates assigned to wrong file (ba.md instead of qa.md only)
failures.recommendation: Remove fail gates from AC2; keep in AC3 only

failures.issue: Investigation incompleteness — third registration site (prompt-workflow.py agent_types) omitted despite BA's own precedent evidence
Spec text vs execution drift — AC10 literal text contradicts its own reference source
failures.recommendation: Correct AC10 to accurately reflect the two distinct EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS purposes

spec_section_updates.section_4: Cycle 1 BA-QA Validation: BA spec ticket-20260527-132200.md produced with 11 ACs. 3 critical defects found: (1) AC2 assigns BA-QA fail gates to agents/ba.md (wrong file — they belong in qa.md per spec Section 5 'BA-QA validation gains new fail gates'); (2) AC5 omits the third required registration location (hooks/prompt-workflow.py agent_types list, confirmed present at line 598, currently lacks 'graphify'); (3) AC10 claims 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' but that reference (lines 35-42) contains /venv/, /worktrees/, /.archive/, /plugins/, /.git/, /__pycache__/, /node_modules/ — NOT .env, credentials, keys, logs. 2 major defects: (4) commands/dev-overnight.md absent from affected files despite spec '/dev-overnight: enabled'; (5) no AC covers Command Coverage table (cp-07 has nothing to verify).
spec_section_updates.section_6: AC2 scope misassignment: spec Section 5 places 'BA-QA validation gains new fail gates' as QA-mode validation rules; BA placed them in agents/ba.md THEN clause which is wrong. AC5 incomplete: BA's own Technical Hints cite 3-site precedent (pretool-cp-checkin.py + spec-check.py + prompt-workflow.py agent_types) but AC5 only covers 2 sites. AC10 drift: 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' is factually incorrect — the reference lacks the security patterns (.env, credentials, keys, logs) listed in the same AC clause. dev-overnight.md absent from 32-file plan: spec says '/dev-overnight: enabled' but no concrete file change or rationale for omission. Command coverage AC gap: spec has explicit coverage table but no AC verifies it.
spec_section_updates.section_7: BA must: (1) Remove 'AND the three new BA-QA fail gates' from AC2 THEN clause — keep fail gates in AC3 only. (2) Add third THEN clause to AC5: 'graphify' appears in agent_types list at hooks/prompt-workflow.py:598. (3) Rewrite AC10 to cite spec Section 5 Risk item 7 and arch-8 as the authority for .env/credentials/keys/logs exclusion; remove false 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' attribution. (4) Explicitly document in Technical Hints whether commands/dev-overnight.md needs graphify insertion points or whether 'enabled, shares global update' means only agent-level changes. (5) Add AC12 for Command Coverage verification covering all 5 commands plus /pull.

refined_context.recommended_approach: BA must issue a revised ticket with 5 specific corrections enumerated above. All 3 critical objections block DEV dispatch.
refined_context.additional_context: All other BA claims verified accurate: fcntl.LOCK_EX confirmed at spec-check.py:171; test-writer precedent at pretool-cp-checkin.py:72 + spec-check.py:59 + prompt-workflow.py:598; 'graphify' confirmed absent from all three locations; blast-radius-tool.py:35 EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS confirmed; scripts/todo/dev.py:1-41 confirmed 15 steps without graphify.

summary: BA spec fails on 5 dimensions: AC2 misassigns QA fail gates to ba.md; AC5 omits the 3rd registration site (prompt-workflow.py:598); AC10 self-contradicts its reference; dev-overnight.md absent from affected files; no AC covers the command coverage table.
